title: 1.1: States the HTTP endpoint path that handles panel queries (such as /api/ds/query path, query endpoint URL, datasource query route)
1.2: States the HTTP method used for panel query requests (such as POST method, POST request type, HTTP POST verb)
1.3: Identifies the data structure format returned in query responses (such as frames format, dataframes structure, schema and values arrays)
1.4: States that repeated query executions are processed identically with no special handling (such as second execution treated the same as first, no cached results, each request hits backend fresh)
1.5: Reports the HTTP status code for successful query responses (such as 200 OK status, success status code, HTTP 200 response)
1.6: Reports whether query processing appears in server logs at default log level (such as queries not logged by default, no query payload in logs, minimal query logging)
1.7: Describes the query payload structure sent to the backend (such as queries array with refId, datasource object with type and uid, time range parameters)
1.8: Identifies the Cache-Control response header and its directive value (such as Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache directive, caching policy header)
1.9: Describes the request flow through Grafana backend (such as browser to backend to datasource, queries proxied through Grafana, backend routes requests to datasource plugin)
